meta.description: Oxford Y4 OSCE linked data. Edit here; Claude mirrors changes to both HTML files.

cvs.name: Cardiovascular
cvs.sub: Pulse character, JVP, the murmur table.

abdo.name: Abdominal
abdo.sub: Organomegaly, CLD stigmata, masses by region.

neuro.name: Neuro (limbs & cranial)
neuro.sub: UMN/LMN split, gait, the cranial-nerve patterns.

neck.name: Neck & Thyroid
neck.sub: Midline manoeuvres, node vs gland, thyroid status.

fbc.name: FBC
fbc.how: Venous sample, EDTA (purple) tube. Reports Hb, WCC + differential, platelets, MCV. Reflex to blood film if any line is abnormal.
fbc.think: Anaemia mimics or worsens almost everything. WCC flags infection. Platelets must be known before anticoagulation. Think FBC whenever the diagnosis could be caused, masked, or made unsafe to treat by a blood abnormality.

obs_spo2.name: Obs / SpO₂
obs_spo2.how: HR, BP, RR, SpO₂, temp → feeds NEWS2. Takes seconds, repeatable.
obs_spo2.think: The first question is never the diagnosis — it's 'is this patient sick right now?' Obs answer that.

u_e.name: U&E
u_e.how: Serum sample. Reports Na, K, urea, creatinine → eGFR, AKI staging. Compare to patient's baseline.
u_e.think: Think U&E when giving a renally-cleared drug, when K could be dangerous, or when the kidney is the victim (heart failure, sepsis, obstruction).

ecg.name: ECG
ecg.how: 12-lead, correct lead placement. Rate, rhythm, axis, P-QRS-T morphology. Free, instant, repeatable.
ecg.think: Any chest symptom, palpitation, collapse, abnormal pulse → ECG before you theorise. Think ECG as the default first move in cardiovascular work.

tfts.name: TFTs
tfts.how: Serum TSH first (most sensitive), free T4 ± T3. TSH moves opposite to thyroid hormone in primary disease.
tfts.think: Thyroid is the great mimic. Think TFTs whenever a presentation is multi-system and otherwise unexplained — and always in new AF or a neck lump.

node_survey.name: Node survey
node_survey.how: Systematic palpation of all regional basins. Soft-mobile-tender = reactive; hard-fixed-painless = malignant.
node_survey.think: Nodes are the drainage map. Think node survey with any lump or suspected malignancy.

fna_biopsy.name: FNA / core biopsy
fna_biopsy.how: FNA = cytology; core = histology (preferred for solid lumps). Often image-guided. Tissue endpoint of triple assessment.
fna_biopsy.think: Imaging tells you a lump's shape; only tissue tells you what it is.

staging_ct.name: Staging CT
staging_ct.how: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast to map spread (TNM). Done after tissue confirms malignancy.
staging_ct.think: Once you know it's cancer, the next question is 'how far has it gone?' — that decides curative vs palliative intent.

bp.name: BP
bp.how: Correct cuff size, supported arm. Check both arms (>20 mmHg → dissection/subclavian) and lying/standing (postural drop).
bp.think: Think asymmetry in dissection/coarctation, postural drop in falls/syncope, four-limb BP for coarctation.

cxr.name: CXR
cxr.how: Erect PA if possible. Systematic read. Cheap, fast, high yield.
cxr.think: Almost every respiratory and cardiac sign earns a CXR. Think CXR as the workhorse first image.

crp.name: CRP
crp.how: Serum inflammatory marker; rises and falls faster than ESR. A trend tracks treatment response.
crp.think: CRP quantifies inflammation. Use it to gauge severity (CURB-65) and track an infection's response.

cap_glucose.name: Capillary glucose
cap_glucose.how: Bedside fingerprick. Instant. The fastest reversible cause to exclude in acute neurology.
cap_glucose.think: Hypo/hyperglycaemia masquerades as stroke, seizure, collapse, confusion. Think glucose first in any altered consciousness.

clotting.name: Clotting
clotting.how: Citrate (blue) tube: PT/INR, APTT, fibrinogen. INR also reflects liver synthetic function.
clotting.think: Before any procedure or anticoagulation, in liver disease, or in any major bleed/sepsis (DIC).

duplex_uss.name: Duplex USS
duplex_uss.how: B-mode + Doppler — shows vessel and flow. Non-invasive, no radiation.
duplex_uss.think: Whenever the question is 'is this vessel flowing, blocked, or refluxing?' Think duplex as the go-to vascular image.

urine_dip.name: Urine dip
urine_dip.how: Bedside multistix: blood, protein, leucocytes, nitrites, glucose, ketones. Cheap, instant.
urine_dip.think: A tiny test touching many systems. Think urine dip as a near-free screen in renal, cardiac and metabolic work.

troponin.name: Troponin
troponin.how: Serial samples (0 and 3–6 h) — the rise/fall matters, not one number. Sensitive but not specific.
troponin.think: Think troponin when myocardium might be dying — but it leaks in PE, sepsis, renal failure too.

bnp.name: BNP
bnp.how: Released when ventricular walls are stretched. Normal BNP makes heart failure unlikely (strong rule-out).
bnp.think: Think BNP as the heart saying 'I'm under strain.' Screen breathlessness for heart failure before committing to echo.

echo.name: Echo
echo.how: Transthoracic first; transoesophageal for valves/vegetations/LA. Gives EF, chamber sizes, valve gradients.
echo.think: Echo is the structural answer. Confirm what BNP screened, size a murmur, hunt vegetations in endocarditis.

coronary_angio.name: Coronary angiogram
coronary_angio.how: Invasive contrast study of the coronaries; can proceed to PCI. The definitive map of coronary disease.
coronary_angio.think: Think angiogram when ischaemic disease is confirmed and you need to know which vessel — and potentially treat it.

abg.name: ABG
abg.how: Radial arterial sample. Reports pH, PaO₂, PaCO₂, HCO₃, lactate. Defines Type 1 vs Type 2 failure and acid-base state.
abg.think: Think ABG when oxygenation or ventilation is in doubt. It quantifies how sick the lungs/metabolism really are.

peak_flow.name: Peak flow
peak_flow.how: Best of three forced blows. Compare to predicted/personal best; serial readings track obstruction and reversibility.
peak_flow.think: Think peak flow in any wheeze — objectifies obstruction and separates asthma from fixed COPD with reversibility test.

sputum.name: Sputum MC&S
sputum.how: Sample for MC&S; add AFB if TB suspected, viral swabs/legionella urinary antigen as indicated.
sputum.think: Think sputum whenever there's a productive cough and you want to name the organism.

ct_chest.name: CT chest
ct_chest.how: Cross-sectional, usually contrast. CTPA protocol for PE. Resolves what CXR can only hint at.
ct_chest.think: Think CT chest when CXR shows something needing characterisation or when PE is suspected (CTPA).

hrct.name: HRCT
hrct.how: Thin-slice, high-detail CT tuned for lung tissue. Shows reticular pattern, honeycombing, traction bronchiectasis.
hrct.think: Think HRCT for parenchymal disease — fibrosis/ILD, bronchiectasis. The pattern names the cause.

spirometry.name: Spirometry
spirometry.how: FEV₁, FVC, ratio. Obstructive (↓ratio) vs restrictive (preserved ratio, ↓volumes). Add reversibility for asthma.
spirometry.think: Think spirometry to classify chronic breathlessness — sorts obstructive from restrictive.

bronchoscopy.name: Bronchoscopy
bronchoscopy.how: Flexible scope to visualise airways, sample (lavage, biopsy), or relieve obstruction.
bronchoscopy.think: Think bronchoscopy when imaging shows a central lesion needing tissue, or for haemoptysis with a visible target.

lfts.name: LFTs
lfts.how: Hepatocellular (↑ALT/AST) vs cholestatic (↑ALP/GGT/bilirubin) pattern; albumin + INR = synthetic function.
lfts.think: Think LFTs in jaundice, organomegaly, alcohol history, and before hepatically-metabolised drugs.

amylase.name: Amylase
amylase.how: Serum; >3× upper limit supports pancreatitis. Lipase is more specific. Always check calcium too.
amylase.think: Think amylase in acute epigastric pain radiating to the back.

abdo_uss.name: Abdominal USS
abdo_uss.how: First-line, no radiation. Excellent for gallstones, biliary tree, liver texture, kidneys, free fluid, AAA size.
abdo_uss.think: Think abdominal USS as the first image for RUQ pain, organomegaly, ascites, AAA screening.

ct_abdomen.name: CT abdomen
ct_abdomen.how: Cross-sectional, usually contrast. Defines obstruction, perforation, masses, collections, bleeding.
ct_abdomen.think: Think CT abdomen when USS is inconclusive or the picture is surgical.

endoscopy.name: Endoscopy
endoscopy.how: OGD (upper) or colonoscopy (lower): direct view + biopsy + therapy. Sigmoidoscopy at Y4 level.
endoscopy.think: Think endoscopy when the mucosa is the answer — dysphagia, haematemesis, change in bowel habit, varices.

b12_folate.name: B12 / folate
b12_folate.how: Serum. Low B12 → subacute combined degeneration (dorsal columns + corticospinal). Check alongside FBC (macrocytosis).
b12_folate.think: Think B12 in peripheral neuropathy, proprioceptive loss, or absent ankle jerks with upgoing plantars.

hba1c.name: HbA1c
hba1c.how: 3-month average glycaemia, one serum sample, no fasting needed.
hba1c.think: Think HbA1c in glove-and-stocking neuropathy, arterial disease, or unexplained multi-system presentation.

ct_mri_brain.name: CT/MRI brain
ct_mri_brain.how: CT first for acute (bleed, fast); MRI for detail (posterior fossa, MS, tumour, early ischaemia).
ct_mri_brain.think: Think brain imaging once the exam localises a lesion centrally — UMN signs, cranial nerve deficits, cerebellar signs.

mri_spine.name: MRI spine
mri_spine.how: Detailed cord and root imaging, no radiation. The test for myelopathy and radiculopathy.
mri_spine.think: Think MRI spine when signs point to cord or root — sensory level, dermatomal loss, mixed UMN/LMN, compression.

nerve_conduction.name: Nerve conduction
nerve_conduction.how: NCS/EMG measures conduction speed and muscle electrical activity. Localises and characterises peripheral lesions.
nerve_conduction.think: Think nerve conduction for carpal tunnel, peripheral neuropathy, suspected MND — to prove and localise the LMN process.

lp.name: LP
lp.how: CSF: opening pressure, cells, protein, glucose, oligoclonal bands. Image first if raised ICP suspected.
lp.think: Think LP for meningitis/encephalitis, subarachnoid (if CT negative), oligoclonal bands in MS.

uss_lump.name: USS
uss_lump.how: Bedside/radiology ultrasound — cystic vs solid, vascularity, depth. No radiation, can guide biopsy.
uss_lump.think: Think USS as the first image of any lump — it answers cystic-vs-solid objectively and guides the needle.

mammogram_uss.name: Mammogram / USS
mammogram_uss.how: USS under 35 (denser breast), mammogram 35+. The imaging arm of triple assessment.
mammogram_uss.think: Think breast imaging as one leg of the triple-assessment stool — never alone: clinical + imaging + biopsy together.

thyroid_ab.name: Thyroid antibodies
thyroid_ab.how: TSH-receptor antibodies → Graves'; anti-TPO → Hashimoto's. Run with TFTs to find the cause.
thyroid_ab.think: Once TFTs tell you the status, antibodies tell you the cause.

ldh.name: LDH
ldh.how: Serum enzyme; non-specific marker of cell turnover/breakdown. Part of haemolysis screen and tumour-bulk marker.
ldh.think: Think LDH when cells are being destroyed or proliferating — haemolysis (with bilirubin) or lymphoma/leukaemia.

thyroid_uss.name: Thyroid USS
thyroid_uss.how: First-line neck imaging. Characterises nodules (size, echogenicity, vascularity, calcification → U-grade).
thyroid_uss.think: Think thyroid USS for any neck swelling that moves on swallowing — to characterise before FNA.

abpi.name: ABPI
abpi.how: Highest ankle systolic ÷ brachial systolic, using Doppler. <0.5 critical, 0.5–0.8 PAD, 0.8–1.3 normal, >1.3 calcified.
abpi.think: Think ABPI to quantify arterial disease — and always before compression of a venous ulcer.

doppler.name: Doppler
doppler.how: Handheld probe to detect flow where pulses are impalpable; assesses venous reflux at SFJ/SPJ.
doppler.think: Think Doppler the moment a pulse can't be felt, or to test valve competence in varicose veins.

d_dimer.name: D-dimer
d_dimer.how: Serum fibrin breakdown product. High sensitivity, low specificity — only useful to rule out when pre-test probability is low.
d_dimer.think: Think D-dimer only after Wells score: low probability + normal D-dimer = no scan needed.

glucose_lipids.name: Glucose / lipids
glucose_lipids.how: Fasting glucose/HbA1c and lipid profile — the modifiable vascular risk factors.
glucose_lipids.think: Think glucose and lipids in any arterial disease — they're the cause you can treat.

ct_angiogram.name: CT angiogram
ct_angiogram.how: Contrast CT mapping the arterial tree. Urgent in acute limb ischaemia and dissection.
ct_angiogram.think: Think CT angiogram when you need the arterial roadmap urgently. Don't let it delay the vascular referral.

angiography.name: Angiography
angiography.how: Catheter contrast study; can be diagnostic and therapeutic (angioplasty/stent).
angiography.think: Think formal angiography when intervention is on the table — it both confirms the lesion and treats it.

blood_cultures.name: Blood cultures
blood_cultures.how: 3 sets from different sites before antibiotics. Incubate 5 days. Sensitivity ~65–70% in bacteraemia.
blood_cultures.think: Think blood cultures in any fever with a cardiac prosthesis/murmur, or when IE is on the differential.

liver_screen.name: Liver screen
liver_screen.how: Hep B/C serology, ANA, ASMA, AMA, LKM antibodies, caeruloplasmin, alpha-1-antitrypsin, ferritin.
liver_screen.think: Think liver screen when CLD is confirmed but the cause is unknown — it changes management.

hep_serology.name: Hep serology
hep_serology.how: HBsAg, HCV antibody, HBcAb. Baseline before treatment; if positive, genotype and viral load.
hep_serology.think: Think hep serology in any unexplained liver disease, especially with risk factors.

afp.name: AFP
afp.how: Alpha-fetoprotein: a tumour marker for HCC. Also raised in pregnancy and some germ-cell tumours.
afp.think: Think AFP in a cirrhotic patient with new hepatomegaly or a hepatic mass on USS — screens for HCC.

blood_film.name: Blood film
blood_film.how: Peripheral blood smear reviewed by haematologist. Identifies cell morphology (spherocytes, blasts, target cells).
blood_film.think: Think blood film when FBC flags an abnormality — it moves from 'something's wrong' to 'what exactly'.

ammonia.name: Serum ammonia
ammonia.how: Serum sample processed immediately (ice). Correlates with encephalopathy grade but not perfectly.
ammonia.think: Think ammonia in any patient with liver disease and reduced consciousness or confusion.

ascitic_tap.name: Ascitic tap
ascitic_tap.how: Diagnostic: fluid for SAAG, protein, cytology, MC&S, LDH. SAAG ≥11 g/L → portal hypertension.
ascitic_tap.think: Think ascitic tap to characterise new ascites — the SAAG tells you portal vs non-portal cause.

uptake_scan.name: Thyroid uptake scan
uptake_scan.how: Tc99m or I123 scintiscan. Hot nodule = benign; cold nodule = higher malignancy risk; diffuse uptake = Graves'.
uptake_scan.think: Think uptake scan when hyperthyroidism needs a cause — Graves' vs toxic nodule vs thyroiditis.

mri_orbits.name: MRI orbits
mri_orbits.how: MRI with fat suppression to assess extraocular muscles and optic nerve compression in Graves' orbitopathy.
mri_orbits.think: Think MRI orbits when exophthalmos is severe or vision is threatened — rules out compressive optic neuropathy.

four_limb_bp.name: Four-limb BP
four_limb_bp.how: BP in all four limbs using appropriate cuff. Upper-high/lower-low difference is pathognomonic of coarctation.
four_limb_bp.think: Think four-limb BP whenever radio-femoral delay is found or coarctation is suspected.

wells_score.name: Wells score
wells_score.how: Clinical probability scoring for DVT or PE. Guides D-dimer use — only order D-dimer if Wells is low.
wells_score.think: Think Wells score before D-dimer or imaging for suspected DVT/PE — it prevents unnecessary scanning.

pituitary_profile.name: Pituitary profile
pituitary_profile.how: GH, IGF-1, prolactin, LH, FSH, cortisol, TSH — assesses pituitary axis function and mass effect.
pituitary_profile.think: Think pituitary profile when a chiasm/sellar lesion is found — it characterises both function and tumour type.

veps.name: VEPs
veps.how: Visual evoked potentials — delayed P100 latency suggests demyelination of the optic nerve.
veps.think: Think VEPs when optic neuritis is suspected clinically but MRI is equivocal — confirms demyelinating conduction delay.

audiometry.name: Audiometry
audiometry.how: Pure-tone audiogram. Air and bone conduction at each frequency. Localises conductive vs sensorineural loss.
audiometry.think: Think audiometry after bedside Weber/Rinne to formally quantify and characterise a hearing loss.

monospot.name: Monospot / EBV serology
monospot.how: Heterophile antibody test (monospot) for EBV. False negative in early disease; EBV-specific serology if negative.
monospot.think: Think monospot in any teenager/young adult with tender cervical lymphadenopathy + pharyngitis + splenomegaly.

triple_assessment.name: Triple assessment
triple_assessment.how: Three components must all be done: clinical exam + imaging (USS<35/mammogram≥35) + core biopsy. No single component is sufficient.
triple_assessment.think: Think triple assessment for any breast lump or nipple change. The combination has >99% sensitivity for carcinoma.

hocm.name: HOCM
hocm.notes: Dynamic obstruction — worsens with reduced preload (standing, Valsalva)

coarctation.name: Coarctation of the aorta
coarctation.notes: Radio-femoral delay; upper-high/lower-low BP; rib notching on CXR

mitral_stenosis.name: Mitral stenosis
mitral_stenosis.notes: Malar flush; rumbling mid-diastolic murmur; AF common

aortic_sclerosis.name: Aortic sclerosis
aortic_sclerosis.notes: Murmur without obstruction; no slow-rising pulse

pleural_effusion.name: Pleural effusion
pleural_effusion.notes: Light's criteria: exudate if protein >35g/L or ratio >0.5

tension_pneumothorax.name: Tension pneumothorax
tension_pneumothorax.management: Immediate needle decompression — do NOT wait for imaging

large_bullae.name: Large bullae
large_bullae.notes: Mimics pneumothorax — do NOT drain without imaging

pulmonary_fibrosis.name: Pulmonary fibrosis / ILD
pulmonary_fibrosis.notes: Basal, bibasal fine crackles; clubbing; reticular HRCT

asthma.name: Asthma
asthma.notes: Reversible airways obstruction; peak flow reversibility >15%

pancoast_tumour.name: Pancoast tumour
pancoast_tumour.notes: Apical lung cancer → Horner's + T1 wasting

chronic_liver_disease.name: Chronic liver disease / cirrhosis
chronic_liver_disease.notes: Spider naevi >5, gynaecomastia, palmar erythema, leukonychia

hepatic_encephalopathy.name: Hepatic encephalopathy
hepatic_encephalopathy.notes: Find and treat precipitant: bleed, infection, constipation, drugs

polycystic_kidneys.name: Polycystic kidneys
polycystic_kidneys.notes: Bilateral, ballotable, can get above; AD inheritance

aaa.name: Abdominal aortic aneurysm
aaa.notes: Pulsatile AND expansile; refer if ≥5.5 cm

inguinal_hernia.name: Inguinal hernia
inguinal_hernia.notes: Above and medial to pubic tubercle; direct vs indirect

femoral_hernia.name: Femoral hernia
femoral_hernia.notes: Below and lateral to pubic tubercle; higher strangulation risk

acute_cholecystitis.name: Acute cholecystitis
acute_cholecystitis.notes: Murphy's sign; USS = first-line; may show gallstones, wall thickening

virchow_malignancy.name: Intra-abdominal malignancy (Virchow's)
virchow_malignancy.notes: Troisier's sign; gastric most common primary

umn_lesion.name: UMN lesion
umn_lesion.notes: Stroke, MS, cord compression, tumour — above anterior horn

lmn_lesion.name: LMN lesion
lmn_lesion.notes: MND, root, plexus, nerve, NMJ, muscle — along motor neuron

cerebellar_disease.name: Cerebellar disease
cerebellar_disease.notes: DANISH: Dysdiadochokinesia, Ataxia, Nystagmus, Intention tremor, Slurred speech, Hypotonia

mnd.name: Motor neurone disease
mnd.notes: Mixed UMN+LMN; no sensory involvement; bulbar features

carpal_tunnel.name: Carpal tunnel syndrome
carpal_tunnel.notes: Median nerve compression at wrist; nocturnal symptoms

common_peroneal_palsy.name: Common peroneal palsy
common_peroneal_palsy.notes: Foot drop; sensory loss dorsum foot

peripheral_neuropathy.name: Peripheral neuropathy
peripheral_neuropathy.notes: Causes: diabetes, alcohol, B12, CKD, drugs, hereditary

optic_neuritis.name: Optic neuritis
optic_neuritis.notes: Most common presentation of MS in young adults

surgical_3rd_nerve.name: Surgical 3rd nerve palsy
surgical_3rd_nerve.notes: PCom aneurysm — pupil INVOLVED; neurosurgical emergency

bells_palsy.name: Bell's palsy
bells_palsy.notes: LMN 7th — forehead involved; exclude other causes first

ramsay_hunt.name: Ramsay Hunt syndrome
ramsay_hunt.notes: VZV reactivation; vesicles in ear canal; worse prognosis than Bell's

conductive_hearing_loss.name: Conductive hearing loss
conductive_hearing_loss.notes: Weber → affected side; Rinne bone>air affected side

aneurysm_peripheral.name: Peripheral aneurysm
aneurysm_peripheral.notes: Expansile AND pulsatile; most common = popliteal

malignancy_lump.name: Malignancy
malignancy_lump.notes: Hard, irregular, tethered; hard fixed regional nodes

inflammatory_breast_ca.name: Inflammatory breast cancer
inflammatory_breast_ca.notes: Peau d'orange; rapid onset; poor prognosis

pagets_nipple.name: Paget's disease of the nipple
pagets_nipple.notes: Associated with underlying DCIS or invasive carcinoma

duct_papilloma.name: Duct papilloma
duct_papilloma.notes: Most common cause of bloody single-duct discharge

thyroid_mass.name: Thyroid mass
thyroid_mass.notes: Moves on swallowing (not on tongue protrusion)

thyroglossal_cyst.name: Thyroglossal cyst
thyroglossal_cyst.notes: Moves on BOTH swallowing AND tongue protrusion — distinguishing feature

graves_disease.name: Graves' disease
graves_disease.notes: TSH-receptor antibodies; exophthalmos is specific to Graves'

hashimotos.name: Hashimoto's thyroiditis
hashimotos.notes: Anti-TPO antibodies; most common cause of hypothyroidism in UK

reactive_lymphadenopathy.name: Reactive lymphadenopathy
reactive_lymphadenopathy.notes: Soft, mobile, tender; usually localised to drainage area

carotid_stenosis.name: Carotid stenosis
carotid_stenosis.notes: Risk of stroke; do NOT palpate hard

neuropathic_ulcer.name: Neuropathic ulcer
neuropathic_ulcer.notes: Painless, over pressure points; normal ABPI

chronic_pad.name: Chronic PAD / claudication
chronic_pad.notes: ABPI 0.5–0.8; shiny hairless skin; calf pain on walking

acute_limb_ischaemia.name: Acute limb ischaemia
acute_limb_ischaemia.management: Urgent vascular referral + CT angiogram; do not delay
acute_limb_ischaemia.notes: The 6 Ps: Pain, Pale, Pulseless, Paraesthetic, Paralysed, Perishingly cold

chronic_venous_insufficiency.name: Chronic venous insufficiency
chronic_venous_insufficiency.notes: Inverted champagne bottle; haemosiderin; ABPI before compression

venous_ulcer.name: Venous ulcer
venous_ulcer.notes: Sloping edge, medial gaiter; check ABPI before compression

dvt.name: DVT
dvt.notes: Wells score + D-dimer to guide imaging

sfj_incompetence.name: SFJ incompetence / varicose veins
sfj_incompetence.notes: Thrill at SFJ; cough impulse; tap test transmits

high_output_states.name: High-output states
high_output_states.notes: Anaemia, thyrotoxicosis, pregnancy, AV fistula → collapsing pulse

fat_necrosis.name: Fat necrosis
fat_necrosis.notes: History of trauma; can mimic carcinoma on imaging

saphena_varix.name: Saphena varix
saphena_varix.notes: Groin lump; cough impulse; blue; disappears on lying

carotid_dissection.name: Carotid dissection
carotid_dissection.notes: Cause of Horner's in young patients

parotid_tumour.name: Parotid tumour
parotid_tumour.notes: LMN 7th nerve palsy — examine parotid in facial palsy

cpa_lesion.name: CPA lesion
cpa_lesion.notes: Acoustic neuroma most common; affects VII and VIII

bakers_cyst.name: Ruptured Baker's cyst
bakers_cyst.notes: Popliteal cyst rupture mimics DVT; USS distinguishes

retinal_disease.name: Retinal disease
retinal_disease.notes: Causes RAPD without optic nerve lesion

signs.label: Pulse
signs.name: Collapsing pulse
signs.cue: “Bounding then suddenly collapses on raising the arm.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: ESM radiating to carotids
signs.cue: “Ejection systolic, URSE, to carotids; slow-rising pulse.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: PSM radiating to axilla
signs.cue: “Pansystolic at apex → axilla, louder in expiration.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Mid-diastolic at apex
signs.cue: “Rumbling, bell, rolled left; malar flush, small pulse.”

signs.label: Hands
signs.name: Splinters / Osler / Janeway
signs.notes: Duke criteria

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Dull, bronchial, ↑VR
signs.cue: “Dull, bronchial breathing, ↑ vocal resonance, central trachea.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Stony dull, absent BS & VR
signs.notes: Light's criteria on pleural fluid

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Hyper-resonant, ↓BS, ↓VR
signs.notes: Tension = clinical diagnosis, decompress without imaging

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Dull, ↓all, trachea TOWARDS
signs.cue: “Dull, ↓ breath sounds & resonance, trachea pulled towards.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Fine end-inspiratory crackles
signs.cue: “Fine Velcro crackles, don’t clear on cough.”

signs.label: Eyes
signs.name: Horner's syndrome
signs.notes: Check hand wasting (T1) — Pancoast triad

signs.label: Palpation
signs.name: Hepatomegaly
signs.cue: “Smooth liver edge 4 cm below costal margin, moves with breathing.”

signs.label: Palpation
signs.name: Splenomegaly
signs.cue: “LUQ mass with notch, can’t get above it, dull, moves on inspiration.”

signs.label: Arms
signs.name: Asterixis (hepatic flap)
signs.notes: Find precipitant: bleed, infection, constipation, drugs

signs.label: Neck
signs.name: Virchow's node
signs.notes: Troisier's sign — Virchow's node = left supraclavicular

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Stoma
signs.notes: RIF + spouted = ileostomy; LIF + flush = colostomy

signs.label: Groin
signs.name: Reducible cough-impulse lump
signs.cue: “Groin lump, cough impulse, reduces lying flat.”

signs.label: Special
signs.name: Murphy's sign
signs.cue: “RUQ palpation in inspiration → catch of breath.”

signs.label: Pattern
signs.name: UMN pattern
signs.cue: “↑tone, brisk reflexes, upgoing plantars, no wasting.”
signs.notes: Stroke, MS, cord compression, tumour

signs.label: Pattern
signs.name: LMN pattern
signs.notes: MND, root, plexus, nerve, NMJ, muscle

signs.label: Cerebellar
signs.name: DANISH signs
signs.notes: DANISH: Dysdiadochokinesia, Ataxia, Nystagmus, Intention tremor, Slurred speech, Hypotonia

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Thenar wasting
signs.notes: Tinel's / Phalen's at wrist; sensory map

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Fasciculations
signs.notes: UMN + LMN + bulbar = MND until proven otherwise

signs.label: Sensation
signs.name: Glove/stocking loss
signs.notes: Causes: diabetes, alcohol, B12, CKD, drugs

signs.label: CN II
signs.name: RAPD
signs.notes: Check acuity, colour, fundoscopy, fields

signs.label: CN III
signs.name: Third nerve palsy
signs.notes: Pupil involved → surgical emergency (aneurysm); pupil spared → microvascular

signs.label: CN VII
signs.name: LMN facial palsy (forehead involved)
signs.cue: “Whole side of face weak incl. forehead.”
signs.notes: Check ear for vesicles (Ramsay Hunt); protect the eye

signs.label: CN VII
signs.name: UMN facial palsy (forehead spared)
signs.notes: Forehead bilaterally innervated → spared in UMN lesions

investigations.note: MRI IAM if asymmetric SN loss

signs.label: Transillumination
signs.name: Transilluminates
signs.notes: Does NOT transilluminate: blood (varicocoele), solid tumour

signs.label: Pulsatility
signs.name: Expansile pulsatile
signs.notes: Pulsatile BUT NOT expansile = transmitted pulsation (not aneurysm)

signs.label: Consistency
signs.name: Hard fixed irregular + hard nodes
signs.notes: Soft mobile tender = benign/reactive until proven otherwise

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Tethering/puckering on arm raise
signs.cue: “Skin puckers over lump as arms raise.”

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Nipple scaling not responding
signs.notes: Eczema spares the nipple; Paget's starts at the nipple

signs.label: Swallow
signs.name: Rises on swallowing
signs.notes: Also percuss retrosternal — retrosternal extension

signs.label: Tongue
signs.name: Rises on tongue protrusion
signs.notes: Sistrunk procedure for excision — must remove hyoid body

signs.label: Palpation
signs.name: Soft mobile tender nodes
signs.notes: Safety-net: if persistent >6 weeks, re-examine

signs.label: Hands/eyes
signs.name: Tremor + warm palms + AF
signs.cue: “Fine tremor, warm moist palms, irregular pulse.”

signs.label: Eyes
signs.name: Exophthalmos / lid lag
signs.notes: Exophthalmos is specific to Graves' — not seen in other causes of hyperthyroidism

signs.label: General
signs.name: Coarse facies + slow-relaxing reflexes
signs.cue: “Dry skin, lost outer eyebrow, slow ankle jerk.”

signs.label: Auscultation
signs.name: Carotid bruit
signs.notes: Do NOT palpate hard; exclude radiating AS murmur

signs.label: Emergency
signs.name: The 6 Ps
signs.cue: “Pain, pale, pulseless, paraesthetic, paralysed, perishingly cold.”
signs.notes: Surgical emergency — do NOT delay vascular referral

signs.label: Inspection
signs.name: Sloping-edge medial ulcer
signs.cue: “Shallow sloping ulcer, sloughy base, medial gaiter.”
signs.notes: LSV = medial; SSV = lateral

signs.label: Special
signs.name: Thrill/cough impulse at SFJ
signs.cue: “Palpable thrill & cough impulse at SFJ; tap test transmits.”
